description: REST API for xAI compatible with other providers.

tools.name: api-key_handle-get-info-request
tools.description: Get information about an API key, including name, status, permissions and users who created or modified this key. Returns array of string.

tools.name: chat_handle-generic-completion-request
tools.description: Create a chat response from text/image chat prompts. This is the endpoint for making requests to chat and image understanding models. Optional: deferred (default: false), frequency_penalty (default: 0), logit_bias (plus 23 more). Returns the new ChatResponse.

params.name: deferred
params.description: If set to `true`, the request returns a `request_id`. You can then get the deferred response by GET `/v1/chat/deferred-completion/{request_id}`.

params.name: frequency_penalty
params.description: (Not supported by reasoning models) Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

params.name: logit_bias
params.description: (Unsupported) A JSON object that maps tokens (specified by their token ID in the tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically, the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

params.name: logprobs
params.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the content of message. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

params.name: max_completion_tokens
params.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, only applies to visible output tokens (i.e. does not apply to tokens used for reasoning or function calls). Defaults to None, meaning the model will generate as many tokens as needed up until the model's maximum context length.

params.name: max_tokens
params.description: [DEPRECATED] The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. Deprecated in favor of `max_completion_tokens`.

params.name: messages
params.description: A list of messages that make up the chat conversation. Different models support different message types, such as image and text.

params.name: model
params.description: Model name for the model to use. Obtainable from <https://console.x.ai/team/default/models> or <https://docs.x.ai/docs/models>.

params.name: n
params.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep n as 1 to minimize costs.

params.name: parallel_tool_calls
params.description: If set to false, the model can perform maximum one tool call.

params.name: presence_penalty
params.description: (Not supported by `grok-3` and reasoning models) Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

params.name: reasoning_effort
params.description: Constrains how hard a reasoning model thinks before responding. Only supported by `grok-4.3`. Possible values are `none` (disables reasoning completely), `low` (this is the default if not specified), `medium` and `high` (uses the most reasoning tokens).

params.name: response_format
params.description: Response format

params.name: search_parameters
params.description: Search parameters

params.name: seed
params.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same `seed` and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the `system_fingerprint` response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: stop
params.description: (Not supported by reasoning models) Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.

params.name: stream
params.description: If set, partial message deltas will be sent.

params.name: stream_options
params.description: Stream options

params.name: temperature
params.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2.

params.name: tool_choice
params.description: Tool choice

params.name: tools
params.description: A list of tools the model may call in JSON-schema. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for. A max of 128 functions are supported.

params.name: top_logprobs
params.description: An integer between 0 and 8 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. logprobs must be set to true if this parameter is used. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

params.name: top_p
params.description: An alternative to sampling with `temperature`, called nucleus sampling, where the model...

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

params.name: web_search_options
params.description: Web search options

tools.name: chat_handle-get-deferred-completion-request
tools.description: Tries to fetch a result for a previously-started deferred completion. Returns `200 Success` with the response body, if the request has been completed. Returns `202 Accepted` when the request is pending processing. Required: request_id.

params.name: request_id
params.description: The deferred request id returned by a previous deferred chat request.

tools.name: complete_handle-generic-request
tools.description: (Legacy - Not supported by reasoning models) Create a text completion response. This endpoint is compatible with the Anthropic API. Optional: max_tokens_to_sample, metadata, model (plus 6 more). Returns the new CompleteResponse.

params.name: max_tokens_to_sample
params.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Metadata

params.name: model
params.description: Model to use for completion.

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompt for the model to perform completion on.

params.name: stop_sequences
params.description: (Not supported by reasoning models) Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.

params.name: stream
params.description: (Unsupported) If set, partial message deltas will be sent. Tokens will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message.

params.name: temperature
params.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2.

params.name: top_k
params.description: (Unsupported) When generating next tokens, randomly selecting the next token from the k most likely options.

params.name: top_p
params.description: An alternative to sampling with `temperature`, called nucleus sampling, where the model...

tools.name: completions_handle-sample-request
tools.description: (Legacy - Not supported by reasoning models) Create a text completion response for a given prompt. Replaced by /v1/chat/completions. Optional: best_of, echo, frequency_penalty (plus 15 more). Returns array of SampleChoice.

params.name: best_of
params.description: (Unsupported) Generates multiple completions internally and returns the top-scoring one. Not functional yet.

params.name: echo
params.description: Option to include the original prompt in the response along with the generated completion.

params.name: frequency_penalty
params.description: (Unsupported) Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

params.name: logit_bias
params.description: (Unsupported) Accepts a JSON object that maps tokens to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. You can use this tokenizer tool to convert text to token IDs. Mathematically, the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

params.name: logprobs
params.description: Include the log probabilities on the `logprobs` most likely output tokens, as well the chosen tokens. For example, if `logprobs` is 5, the API will return a list of the 5 most likely tokens. The API will always return the logprob of the sampled token, so there may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

params.name: max_tokens
params.description: Limits the number of tokens that can be produced in the output. Ensure the sum of prompt tokens and `max_tokens` does not exceed the model's context limit.

params.name: model
params.description: Specifies the model to be used for the request.

params.name: n
params.description: Determines how many completion sequences to produce for each prompt. Be cautious with its use due to high token consumption; adjust `max_tokens` and stop sequences accordingly.

params.name: presence_penalty
params.description: (Not supported by `grok-3` and reasoning models) Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

params.name: prompt
params.description: Input for generating completions, which can be a string, list of strings, token list, or list of token lists. `<|endoftext|>` is used as a document separator, implying a new context start if omitted.

params.name: seed
params.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the system_fingerprint response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

params.name: stop
params.description: (Not supported by reasoning models) Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.

params.name: stream
params.description: Whether to stream back partial progress. If set, tokens will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message.

params.name: stream_options
params.description: Stream options

params.name: suffix
params.description: (Unsupported) Optional string to append after the generated text.

params.name: temperature
params.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both.

params.name: top_p
params.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with `top_p` probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

tools.name: documents_handle-search-request-v2
tools.description: Search for content related to the query within the given collections. Required: query, source. Optional: filter, group_by, instructions (plus 3 more). Returns array of SearchMatch.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional metadata filter string to apply to search results. Uses AIP-160 filter syntax for querying document metadata. Supports comparison operators: `=`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=` Supports logical operators: `AND`, `OR` Supports range syntax: `field:10..20` (inclusive) Examples: `author = 'John'` or `year > 2020 AND category = 'finance'`

params.name: group_by
params.description: Group by

params.name: instructions
params.description: User-defined instructions to be included in the search query. Defaults to generic search instructions.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of chunks to return. Will always return the top matching chunks. Optional, defaults to 10.

params.name: query
params.description: The query to search for which will be embedded using the same embedding model as the one used for the source to query.

params.name: ranking_metric
params.description: Ranking metric

params.name: retrieval_mode
params.description: Retrieval mode

params.name: source-collection-ids
params.description: The collection IDs to search in.

params.name: source-rag-pipeline
params.description: Rag pipeline

tools.name: embedding-models_handle-get-request
tools.description: Get full information about an embedding model with its model_id. Required: model_id. Returns the EmbeddingModel.

params.name: model_id
params.description: ID of the model to get.

tools.name: embedding-models_handle-list-request
tools.description: List all embedding models available to the authenticating API key with full information. Additional information compared to /v1/models includes modalities, fingerprint and alias(es). Returns array of EmbeddingModel.

tools.name: embeddings_handle-request
tools.description: Create an embedding vector representation corresponding to the input text. This is the endpoint for making requests to embedding models. Optional: dimensions, encoding_format, input (plus 3 more). Returns array of Embedding.

params.name: dimensions
params.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have.

params.name: encoding_format
params.description: The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either `float` or `base64`.

params.name: input
params.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or list of tokens. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass a list of strings or list of token lists. The total tokens in the input must not exceed the context window for the model and cannot be an empty string. If the input is a list, the maximum length of the list is 128.

params.name: model
params.description: ID of the model to use.

params.name: preview
params.description: Flag to use the new format of the API.

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

tools.name: files_handle-delete-request
tools.description: Delete a file by ID. After this returns, the file no longer appears in `GET /v1/files`, content download returns 404, and the ID can no longer be referenced in chat attachments. Required: file_id. Destructive.

params.name: file_id
params.description: The file's `id` to delete.

tools.name: files_handle-list-request
tools.description: List files owned by the authenticated team, paginated. The response always returns a `pagination_token`; pass it back as a query parameter to fetch the next page. The end of the list is reached when the returned `data` array is shorter than `limit`. Optional: limit, order, sort_by (plus 3 more).

params.name: limit
params.description: The maximum number of objects to be returned in a single response.

params.name: order
params.description: The ordering to sort the returned files. Use `asc` for ascending and `desc` for descending order.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: The field to sort by. Valid options: `created_at`, `filename`, `size`. Defaults to `created_at`.

params.name: pagination_token
params.description: The pagination token returned by the previous list files request.

params.name: after
params.description: Only included for compatibility. Use `pagination_token` instead.

params.name: filter
params.description: AIP-160 filter expression to narrow down results. **Filterable fields:** | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | `name` (or `file_name`) | string | Fuzzy match on filename | | `file_id` | string | Exact match on file ID | | `size_bytes` | integer | File size in bytes | | `content_type` | string | Partial match on MIME type (e.g. `'pdf'` matches `'application/pdf'`) | | `created_at` | timestamp | RFC 3339 timestamp (e.g. `'2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'`) | | `expires_at` | timestamp | RFC 3339 timestamp | | `upload_status` | string | Upload status (`'Complete'`) | | `user_defined_id` | string | Exact match on user-defined ID | **Operators:** `=`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=` **Logical:** `AND`, `OR`, `NOT` **Examples:** - `name:'quarterly report'` — fuzzy match on filename - `content_type = 'pdf'` — files with PDF content type - `size_bytes > 1000000 AND created_at > '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'` — files larger than 1 MB created after Jan 1, 2024 - `file_id = 'file_abc123'` — exact file ID match

tools.name: files_handle-retrieve-request
tools.description: Retrieve metadata for a single file by ID. Errors with 404 if the file doesn't exist, has been deleted, or has passed its `expires_at`. Required: file_id. Returns the File.

params.name: file_id
params.description: The file's `id` returned by upload or list.

tools.name: files_handle-upload-request
tools.description: Upload a file to xAI's storage. Returns the file's metadata. Files can be referenced by ID anywhere a `file_id` is accepted (e.g. chat attachments). Maximum file size: 50 MB. Files are kept until you delete them, or until `expires_after` elapses if set at upload time. Required: file. Optional: expires_after, purpose.

params.name: expires_after
params.description: Optional TTL in seconds (measured from upload time). Must be between 3600 (1 hour) and 2592000 (30 days). If unset the file does not expire. Accepts either a plain integer or the OpenAI SDK deepObject form (`expires_after[anchor]=created_at` + `expires_after[seconds]=N`) as separate multipart fields. The anchor+seconds form must arrive before the `file` part.

params.name: file
params.description: The file to upload. The filename from the multipart `Content-Disposition: filename=` header is recorded as the file's `filename`.

params.name: purpose
params.description: Optional purpose label, accepted for OpenAI SDK compatibility. xAI does not enforce or interpret this field. Setting `'assistants'` is the conventional choice.

tools.name: files_content_handle-download-file-request
tools.description: Download the contents of a file as a stream of raw bytes. The response `Content-Type` is `application/octet-stream`. Use this for the binary payload; use `GET /v1/files/{file_id}` for metadata only. Required: file_id. Optional: format.

params.name: file_id
params.description: The file's `id` to download.

params.name: format
params.description: Format of the downloaded content.

tools.name: image-generation-models_handle-get-request
tools.description: Get full information about an image generation model with its model_id. Required: model_id. Returns the ImageGenerationModel.

params.name: model_id
params.description: ID of the model to get.

tools.name: image-generation-models_handle-list-request
tools.description: List all image generation models available to the authenticating API key with full information. Additional information compared to /v1/models includes modalities, fingerprint and alias(es). Returns array of ImageGenerationModel.

tools.name: images_handle-edit-request
tools.description: Edit an image based on a prompt. This is the endpoint for making edit requests to image generation models. Required: prompt. Optional: aspect_ratio, image, images (plus 6 more). Returns array of GeneratedImage.

params.name: aspect_ratio
params.description: Aspect ratio

params.name: image
params.description: Image

params.name: images
params.description: List of input images for multi-reference editing. Mutually exclusive with `image`. When multiple images are provided, refer to them as <IMAGE_0>, <IMAGE_1>, etc. in the prompt.

params.name: model
params.description: Model to be used.

params.name: n
params.description: Number of image edits to be generated.

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompt for image editing.

params.name: resolution
params.description: Resolution

params.name: response_format
params.description: Response format to return the image in. Can be `url` or `b64_json`. If `b64_json` is specified, the image will be returned as a base64-encoded string instead of a url to the generated image file.

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

tools.name: images_handle-generate-request
tools.description: Generate an image based on a prompt. This is the endpoint for making generation requests to image generation models. Optional: aspect_ratio, model, n (default: 1) (plus 5 more). Returns array of GeneratedImage.

params.name: aspect_ratio
params.description: Aspect ratio

params.name: model
params.description: Model to be used.

params.name: n

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompt for image generation.

params.name: resolution
params.description: Resolution

params.name: response_format
params.description: Response format to return the image in. Can be url or b64_json. If b64_json is specified, the image will be returned as a base64-encoded string instead of a url to the generated image file.

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

tools.name: me_handle-get-request

params.name: tool_choice
params.description: Tool choice

params.name: tools
params.description: A list of tools the model may call in JSON-schema. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for. A max of 128 functions are supported.

params.description: (Unsupported) When generating next tokens, randomly selecting the next token from the k most likely options.

params.name: top_p

tools.name: models_handle-get-request
tools.description: Get information about a model with its model_id, including pricing. Required: model_id. Returns the Model.

params.name: model_id

tools.name: models_handle-list-request
tools.description: List all models available to the authenticating API key, including model names (ID), creation times, and pricing. Returns array of Model.

tools.name: responses_handle-compact-request
tools.description: The client sends its current input (the same items that would be passed to `POST /v1/responses`) and receives a compacted output window. The output should be used **verbatim** as the `input` of the next `/v1/responses` call (appending only the new user turn). This generalizes the compaction approach used by the coding-agent harness (`generate_session_compact` in xai-grok-shell): 1. Strip tool-result noise from the history. 2. Ask the model to produce a structured ` ` of the conversation. 3. Rebuild a compact window: system message → summary → last user query. 4. Return that window to the client. Required: input, model. Returns array of ModelInputPart.

params.name: input
params.description: The full input window to compact.

params.name: model
params.description: Model to use for compaction summarization (required).

tools.name: responses_handle-delete-stored-completion-request
tools.description: Delete a previously generated response. Required: response_id. Returns the DeleteStoredCompletionResponse. Destructive.

params.name: response_id
params.description: The response id returned by a previous create response request.

tools.name: responses_handle-generic-model-request
tools.description: Generates a response based on text or image prompts. The response ID can be used to retrieve the response later or to continue the conversation without repeating prior context. New responses will be stored for 30 days and then permanently deleted. Required: input. Optional: background (default: false), context_management, include (plus 22 more). Returns the new ModelResponse.

params.name: background
params.description: (Unsupported) Whether to process the response asynchronously in the background.

params.name: context_management
params.description: Optional context-management directives (e.g. compaction). Parsed but not yet executed.

params.name: include
params.description: What additional output data to include in the response. Currently the only supported value is `reasoning.encrypted_content` which returns an encrypted version of the reasoning tokens.

params.name: input
params.description: The input passed to the model. Can be text, image or file.

params.name: instructions
params.description: An alternate way to specify the system prompt. Note that this cannot be used alongside `previous_response_id`, where the system prompt of the previous message will be used.

params.name: logprobs
params.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the content of message. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

params.name: max_output_tokens
params.description: Max number of tokens that can be generated in a response. This includes both output and reasoning tokens.

params.name: max_turns
params.description: Maximum number of agentic tool calling turns allowed for this request. If not set, defaults to the server's global cap. This parameter will be ignored for any non-agentic requests.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Not supported. Only maintained for compatibility reasons.

params.name: model
params.description: Model name for the model to use. Obtainable from <https://console.x.ai/team/default/models> or <https://docs.x.ai/docs/models>.

params.name: parallel_tool_calls
params.description: Whether to allow the model to run parallel tool calls.

params.name: previous_response_id
params.description: The ID of the previous response from the model.

params.name: prompt_cache_key
params.description: Plumbed to x-grok-conv-id for Open Responses compatibility, used for routing.

params.name: reasoning
params.description: Reasoning

params.name: search_parameters
params.description: Search parameters

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: store
params.description: Whether to store the input message(s) and model response for later retrieval.

params.description: If set, partial message deltas will be sent.

params.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2.

params.name: top_logprobs
params.description: An integer between 0 and 8 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. logprobs must be set to true if this parameter is used. Not supported by models `grok-4.20` and newer; the field will be silently ignored if set.

params.description: An alternative to sampling with `temperature`, called nucleus sampling, where the model...

params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help xAI to monitor and detect abuse.

tools.name: responses_input-items_handle-list
tools.description: Returns a paginated list of the input items that were part of the conversation context for the given response. Required: response_id. Optional: limit, order, after.

params.name: response_id

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-100, default 20).

params.name: order
params.description: Sort order: asc or desc. Default asc.

params.name: after
params.description: Cursor for pagination. Returns items after this item ID.

tools.name: skills_handle-delete-request
tools.description: Handle delete request. Required: skill_id. Returns the DeletedSkill. Destructive.

params.description: The skill ID.

params.name: after

params.name: order

tools.name: skills_handle-retrieve-request
tools.description: Handle retrieve request. Required: skill_id. Returns the Skill.

params.name: skill_id
params.description: The skill ID.

tools.name: skills_handle-upload-request
tools.description: Handle upload request. Required: files. Returns the new Skill.

params.name: files
params.description: Skill zip file or one file from a directory upload. Clients may send this field once with a zip file, or repeatedly with directory files.

tools.name: skills_content_handle-download-skill-request
tools.description: Handle download skill request. Required: skill_id.

params.name: skill_id
params.description: The skill ID.

tools.name: tokenize-text_handle-request
tools.description: Tokenize text with the specified model. Optional: model, text, user. Returns array of TokenizeResponseToken.

params.name: model
params.description: The model to tokenize with.

params.name: text
params.description: The text content to be tokenized.

params.name: user
params.description: Optional user identifier.

tools.name: video-generation-models_handle-get-request
tools.description: Get full information about a video generation model with its model_id. Required: model_id. Returns the VideoGenerationModel.

params.name: model_id
params.description: ID of the model to get.

tools.name: video-generation-models_handle-list-request
tools.description: List all video generation models available to the authenticating API key with full information. Returns array of VideoGenerationModel.

tools.name: videos_handle-edit-request
tools.description: Edit a video based on a prompt. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a request_id for polling. Required: prompt, video. Optional: model, output, service_tier (plus 1 more).

params.name: model
params.description: Model to be used.

params.name: output
params.description: Output

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompt for video editing.

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user.

params.name: video-file-id
params.description: File ID from the xAI Files API. Mutually exclusive with `url`. The file must be a video (e.g., MP4) and fully uploaded.

params.name: video-url
params.description: URL of the video (public URL or base64-encoded data URL). The video must have the `.mp4` file extension and be encoded with `.mp4` supported codecs such as H.265, H.264, AV1, etc. Required when `file_id` is not set.

tools.name: videos_handle-extend-request
tools.description: Extend a video by generating continuation content. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a request_id for polling. Required: prompt, video. Optional: duration, model, output (plus 1 more).

params.name: duration
params.description: Duration of the extension segment to generate in seconds (1-10). Defaults to 6 seconds if not specified.

params.name: model
params.description: Model to be used.

params.name: output
params.description: Output

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompt describing what should happen next in the video.

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: video-file-id
params.description: File ID from the xAI Files API. Mutually exclusive with `url`. The file must be a video (e.g., MP4) and fully uploaded.

params.name: video-url
params.description: URL of the video (public URL or base64-encoded data URL). The video must have the `.mp4` file extension and be encoded with `.mp4` supported codecs such as H.265, H.264, AV1, etc. Required when `file_id` is not set.

tools.name: videos_handle-generate-request
tools.description: Generate a video from a text prompt and optionally an image. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a request_id for polling. Optional: aspect_ratio, duration (default: 8), image (plus 7 more).

params.name: aspect_ratio
params.description: Aspect ratio

params.name: duration
params.description: Video duration in seconds. Range: [1, 15]. Default: 8. Also accepts `seconds` for OpenAI API compatibility. Accepts both number (8) and string ('8') values.

params.name: image
params.description: Image

params.name: model
params.description: Model to be used.

params.name: output
params.description: Output

params.name: prompt
params.description: Prompt for video generation. Required for text-to-video (T2V) and reference-to-video (R2V). Optional for image-to-video (I2V) — when omitted, the model generates a video from the image alone.

params.name: reference_images
params.description: Optional reference images for reference-to-video (R2V) generation. When provided generates video using these images as style/content references.

params.name: resolution
params.description: Resolution

params.name: service_tier
params.description: Service tier

params.name: user
params.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user.

tools.name: videos_handle-get-deferred-request
tools.description: Returns the current status of a video generation job. When the job completes successfully the response contains the generated video URL. When the job fails the response contains a structured `error` object with a machine-readable `code` and a human-readable `message`. Both successful and failed completions return HTTP 200 — use the `status` field (`'done'` or `'failed'`) to distinguish between the two. Required: request_id.

params.name: request_id
params.description: The deferred request id returned by a previous video generation request.
